LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANTS WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. AND SHEILA SANTANA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING (GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. GRANT AS TO DISCRIMINATION AND WRONGFUL DISCHARGE CAUSE OF ACTION. THE FAILURE TO TRAIN AND THE WARNING ARE NOT ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTIONS. DEFENDANT HAD GOOD CAUSE TO DISCHARGE PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT SUBSTANTIAL RESPONSIVE EVIDENCE THAT DISCHARGE WAS PRETEXTUAL. UNDER HORN V. CUSHMAN & WAKEFIELD, (1999) 72 CAL.APP.4TH 798, 809, SANTANA'S COMMENTS ARE ENTITLED TO LITTLE WEIGHT BECAUSE THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT SANTANA WAS INVOLVED IN THE DECISION-MAKING PROCESS. THE FAILURE TO PREVENT DISCRIM CAUSE ACTION FAILS BECAUSE THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF DISCRIMINATION. GRANT AS TO BREACH OF CONTRACT AND BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT. PLAINTIFF AT-WILL EMPLOYEE AND NO EVIDENCE OF GOOD CAUSE DISCHARGE COVENANT. GRANT AS TO DEFAMATION. NO EVIDENCE OF DEFAMATORY REMARK. GRANT AS TO INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. NO OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT). PLTF'S ORAL MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/AJR/PB) COURT REPORTER JOE VICKSTEIN, CSR#4780

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. and SHEILA SANTANA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES:
APPEAR: David S. Secrest, Esq. 650/726-7461 for pltf & opposing party; Theodora R. Lee, Esq. & Troy A. Valdez, Esq. 433-1940 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling (GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. GRANT AS TO DISCRIMINATION AND WRONGFUL DISCHARGE CAUSE OF ACTION. THE FAILURE TO TRAIN AND THE WARNING ARE NOT ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTIONS. DEFENDANT HAD GOOD CAUSE TO DISCHARGE PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT SUBSTANTIAL RESPONSIVE EVIDENCE THAT DISCHARGE WAS PRETEXTUAL. UNDER Horn v. Cushman & Wakefield, (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 798, 809, SANTANA'S COMMENTS ARE ENTITLED TO LITTLE WEIGHT BECAUSE THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT SANTANA WAS INVOLVED IN THE DECISION-MAKING PROCESS. THE FAILURE TO PREVENT DISCRIM CAUSE ACTION FAILS BECAUSE THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF DISCRIMINATION. GRANT AS TO BREACH OF CONTRACT AND BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT. PLAINTIFF AT-WILL EMPLOYEE AND NO EVIDENCE OF GOOD CAUSE DISCHARGE COVENANT. GRANT AS TO DEFAMATION. NO EVIDENCE OF DEFAMATORY REMARK. GRANT AS TO INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. NO OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT). Pltf's oral motion to amend complaint denied. Order signed in open court. (302/AJR/PB) Court Reporter Joe Vickstein, CSR#4780
